task: T2 Midstream Adversarial Followups — M1 + C5 + C6 + H3 + M3

T2-S1-followup-A.description: Extend Law 5 antibody to cover causality=None / {} / string / {status:None}; consider contract hardening at src/contracts/snapshot_ingest_contract.py:56-59 to require isinstance(dict)+non-empty-string status.

T2-S1-followup-B.description: Audit src/state/db.py::init_schema for settlements-column parity with live DB (pm_bin_lo/pm_bin_hi/unit/settlement_source_type missing from fresh CREATE); decide canonicalize vs document-as-legacy.

T2-S1-followup-M.description: Two-part historical-data migration. (a) Backfill 1,561 legacy settlement rows with canonical physical_quantity ('mx2t6_local_calendar_day_max') OR document mixed-rows state as permanent + add canonical-only WHERE-clause convention. (b) HKO HIGH retroactive settlement_value audit — query legacy HKO HIGH rows for rounding-flip candidates (raw temperature in [x.5, x+1) range where WMO half-up rounds UP and oracle_truncate rounds DOWN), compute delta under both rules, flag any winning_bin impact, enumerate affected positions/calibration_pairs. Pre-C5 harvester HIGH branch used round_wmo_half_up_value regardless of city; post-C5 uses SettlementSemantics.for_city(city_obj).round_values which applies HKO oracle_truncate. Part (b) added 2026-04-24 per con-nyx T2-S2 finding 2.
T2-S1-followup-M.scope: src/state/** + data migration packet + potentially retroactive calibration_pairs scrub

T2-S3-followup-SCRIPTS.description: Audit training-path scripts in scripts/ for H3 compliance before live-readiness B3 flag flip. con-nyx T2-S3 review identified 4 name-suspicious canonical-path scripts (from 27 bare-JOIN files in scripts/) that must be audited first: (1) scripts/bridge_oracle_to_calibration.py:52 — named calibration bridge, P0 concern; (2) scripts/backfill_observations_from_settlements.py:34 — obs feeds training, P0 concern; (3) scripts/backfill_ens.py:43 — ensemble backfill; (4) scripts/backfill_wu_daily_all.py:331 — WU daily backfill. For each: confirm whether the call path is canonical training or audit/one-off; if canonical, patch the SQL with temperature_metric predicate AND promote the script into H3 rule allowlist; then fail-closed the H3 rule on the promoted scripts. VERIFIED ZERO bare JOIN in refit_platt_v2.py + rebuild_calibration_pairs_v2.py (they read calibration_pairs_v2, not settlements directly).
T2-S3-followup-SCRIPTS.scope: scripts/ training-path audit + semantic_linter.py allowlist extension + tests/test_semantic_linter.py
T2-S3-followup-SCRIPTS.origin: T2-S3 scope decision + con-nyx T2-S3 finding 1

T2-S3-followup-INDIRECT-SQL.description: H3 rule currently scopes to SQL literal args of .execute*/.read_sql* calls. If a future codepath builds SQL via string variables then passes to execute, the H3 rule will miss the violation. Extend _sql_call_literal_args coverage OR add a secondary file-level SQL-string scan. No observed instances today; track-only unless a violation is found.

T2-S4-followup-ALIAS-DROP.description: Drop the deprecation alias CANONICAL_DATA_VERSIONS = CANONICAL_ENSEMBLE_DATA_VERSIONS in src/contracts/ensemble_snapshot_provenance.py once external callers (if any) have migrated. All 4 internal test callsites updated in T2-S4 commit; no production caller uses the alias. Safe to drop at any time; scheduled as a cleanup slice to avoid mid-session refactor churn.

T2-S4-followup-OBSERVATION-WIRING.description: Wire CANONICAL_OBSERVATION_DATA_VERSIONS into src/data/observation_instants_v2_writer.py as a pre-INSERT assertion (pattern analogous to assert_data_version_allowed for ensemble snapshots). This closes the M3 namespace disambiguation from constant-only scaffolding to enforced contract on the observation writer. Owner should verify against live DB that all 1.8M pre-existing observation_instants_v2 rows carry v1.wu-native (confirmed via SELECT DISTINCT 2026-04-24) OR extend allowlist.
T2-S4-followup-OBSERVATION-WIRING.origin: T2-S4 scope decision — observation allowlist scaffolding

T2-S4-followup-SETTLEMENT-WIRING.description: Wire CANONICAL_SETTLEMENT_DATA_VERSIONS into src/execution/harvester.py write path as pre-INSERT assertion. Current harvester uses _HARVESTER_LIVE_DATA_VERSION dispatch dict — the new allowlist gives a single citable constant for the harvester + any future non-harvester settlement writer (P-E reconstruction, backfill paths) to share.
T2-S4-followup-SETTLEMENT-WIRING.origin: T2-S4 scope decision — settlement allowlist scaffolding
